---
title: "Week 1 Synthesis / Exam-1 Practice Readiness"
subtitle: "The whole pipeline, end to end"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Mirrors the 50-pt Week 1 closeout WITHOUT reusing
its secure prompt/data — use a fresh generic dataset. Scope: no
if/else, loops, lists, functions.
============================================================ -->

## The Week 1 pipeline

<!-- TODO: left-to-right diagram or numbered flow:
     text intake -> cast to numbers -> compute limits/gaps ->
     boundary checks -> combine with and/or/not -> readiness decision ->
     written closeout note. Link each stage to its day sheet. -->

1. **Text intake** — readings arrive as strings → [Day 3](03_types_casting_truthiness.qmd)
2. **Cast** to numbers (non-mutating) → [Day 3](03_types_casting_truthiness.qmd)
3. **Compute** limits / gaps from expressions → [Day 2](02_variables_expressions_state.qmd)
4. **Boundary checks** (inclusive edges, separate) → [Day 4](04_comparisons_boundaries.qmd)
5. **Combine** checks with `and` / `or` / `not` → [Day 5](05_boolean_combinations.qmd)
6. **Decide** readiness + name the controlling condition → [Day 5](05_boolean_combinations.qmd)
7. **Write** the closeout note → [Traps sheet frame](06_traps_and_evidence.qmd)

## Closeout section self-check

<!-- TODO: turn the assessment's sections into a student self-check
     (skill + "can I do this?" + which sheet to revisit). -->

| Skill area | Can I…? | Revisit |
|---|---|---|
| Output & state | …separate print / display / store? | Day 1 |
| Types / casts / truthiness | …cast without mutating; reject `bool(label)` as approval? | Day 3 |
| Boundary & Boolean trace | …handle the exact edge; combine with `and`? | Days 4–5 |
| Test-case reasoning | …build a below/exact/above test? | Day 4 |
| Completion & debugging | …fix an unsafe `or`; complete an all-required expression? | Day 5 |
| Closeout explanation | …name the decision, cite evidence, state next action? | Traps |

## Self-assessment bands

<!-- TODO: student-facing "secure / developing / needs-support" per skill,
     each pointing to a next-practice sheet. Adapt from the key's routing
     WITHOUT copying point cutoffs verbatim. -->

## End-to-end worked mini-trace

<!-- TODO: one FRESH generic dataset (not the quiz's pump values) run through
     all seven stages, ending in a completed closeout sentence. -->

```{python}
#| eval: false
# TODO generic example: intake text -> cast -> limits -> checks ->
# combine -> readiness -> one-sentence closeout
```

## Scope guardrails

- No `if/else`, loops, lists, or functions.
- A correct final answer is **not enough** — show trace / test / debug / explanation.
